task_completion.description: Navigate to the Ratings management page in the Magento Admin panel.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Magento Admin panel is visible, specifically showing the 'Ratings' page (usually accessed via Stores > Attributes > Rating).

task_completion.description: Enter 'Fit Accuracy' as the title for the first rating.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'New Rating' or 'Edit Rating' form is visible with 'Fit Accuracy' entered into the 'Default Value' field under the Rating Title section.

task_completion.description: Configure the settings for the 'Fit Accuracy' rating.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The rating form for 'Fit Accuracy' shows 'Default Store View' selected in the 'Visibility In' list, the 'Is Active' checkbox is checked, and '10' is entered in the 'Sort Order' field.

task_completion.description: Enter 'Material Quality' as the title for the second rating.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'New Rating' or 'Edit Rating' form is visible with 'Material Quality' entered into the 'Default Value' field under the Rating Title section.

task_completion.description: Configure the settings for the 'Material Quality' rating.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The rating form for 'Material Quality' shows 'Default Store View' selected in the 'Visibility In' list, the 'Is Active' checkbox is checked, and '20' is entered in the 'Sort Order' field.

task_completion.description: Verify both ratings are saved and appear in the Ratings grid.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Ratings grid/list is visible showing both 'Fit Accuracy' and 'Material Quality' as entries. A green success message like 'You saved the rating.' may also be visible.

integrity.description: The agent used the Magento Admin web interface to create the ratings.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with a web browser displaying the Magento Admin UI, rather than terminal commands or API scripts.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the application by directly inserting records into the database.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no screenshots of database clients (like phpMyAdmin or MySQL CLI) being used to run INSERT queries for the ratings.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with a real browser session.
integrity.visual_evidence: Browser elements (address bar, tabs) and Magento UI elements (menus, buttons, forms) are visible and properly rendered.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires creating two custom product rating criteria in Magento via Stores > Attributes > Rating. 1. 'Fit Accuracy': Must be Active, assigned to 'Default Store View' (Store ID 1), with Sort Order = 10. 2. 'Material Quality': Must be Active, assigned to 'Default Store View' (Store ID 1), with Sort Order = 20.
